response.content:
# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

request.stdin: SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('products') LIMIT 10;

response.stdout:
name,sql
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"

request.stdin: SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand='Fischer' AND series LIKE '%Outdoor SX NT9-HP1%' AND name='Nut Bolt and Washer' AND json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type')='machine screw' AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='10 mm' AND json_extract(properties,'$.length')='50 mm' LIMIT 5;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, category_id, json_extract(properties, '$.fastener_type') AS fastener_type FROM products WHERE brand = 'Heco' AND series LIKE '%Heco Unix HECO 2VD-VNA Nut Bolt and Washer%' AND model = 'HECO 2VD-VNA' AND (name = 'Nut Bolt and Washer' OR category_id LIKE '%nut%bolt%washer%') AND json_extract(properties, '$.fastener_type') = 'threaded rod' LIMIT 10;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type') AS storage_type FROM products WHERE brand='Festool' AND (series LIKE '%Stackable SYS 3JJ-9LM%' OR model='3JJ-9LM' OR name LIKE '%Tool Box and Bag%') LIMIT 50;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

)"

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Catalogue Count Reporting Update

Operating day: 2025-06-22
Workflow: catalogue product-kind count questions
Requested product kind: Nut Bolt and Washer
Requested kind_id: nuts_bolts_washers

For today's count report, count only catalogue SKUs for the requested product
kind that have at least one current inventory row in an open PowerTool store in
Vienna with available_today greater than 0. Count each SKU once even if it is
available in multiple Vienna branches.

request.stdin: SELECT sku, path, json_extract(properties,'$.tool_type') AS tool_type, json_extract(properties,'$.length') AS length FROM products WHERE brand='Gedore' AND model='GED 1CA-RS0' AND name LIKE 'Gedore Red GED 1CA-RS0 Hammer Measuring and Cutting Tool%' AND json_extract(properties,'$.tool_type')='chisel' ORDER BY length;
SELECT DISTINCT category_id FROM products ORDER BY category_id LIMIT 200;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.color_family') AS color_family, json_extract(properties,'$.size') AS size, json_extract(properties,'$.fit') AS fit FROM products WHERE brand='Mascot' AND series LIKE '%Mascot Unique ADV%' AND model='2RT-ZFG' AND name='Work Jacket' AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='Orange' AND json_extract(properties,'$.fit')='slim' LIMIT 20;

request.stdin: SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('products','inventory','stores') ORDER BY name;

response.stdout:
name,sql
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

SELECT p.path FROM products p WHERE p.brand = 'Gedore' COLLATE NOCASE AND p.model = 'GED 1CA-RS0' LIMIT 20;

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS sku_count
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id = 'nuts_bolts_washers'
  AND s.city = 'Vienna'
  AND s.is_open = 1
  AND i.available_today > 0;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

name,sql
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

# Catalogue Count Reporting Update

Operating day: 2024-07-17
Workflow: catalogue product-kind count questions
Requested product kind: Wiper Blade
Requested kind_id: wiper_blades

For today's count report, count only catalogue SKUs for the requested product
kind that have at least one current inventory row in an open PowerTool store in
Vienna with available_today greater than 0. Count each SKU once even if it is
available in multiple Vienna branches.

request.stdin:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS cnt, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT p.path) AS paths
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id = 'valves_connectors'
  AND s.city = 'Graz'
  AND s.is_open = 1
  AND i.available_today > 0;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

request.stdin:
WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT id AS store_id, name, path
  FROM stores
  WHERE name LIKE '%Salzburg%' AND name LIKE '%Elisabeth%Vorstadt%'
), wanted(label, brand, series_like, model, pname, f1k, f1v, f2k, f2v, f3k, f3v) AS (
  VALUES
  ('bahco_pliers','Bahco','%Bahco Comfort Grip%','BE 1ZR-PGS','Pliers and Wrenches','tool_type','side cutter','length','300 mm',NULL,NULL),
  ('mellerud_cleaning','Mellerud','%Mellerud Bio%','MEL 233-MOB','Cleaning Liquid','cleaner_type','multi surface cleaner',NULL,NULL,NULL,NULL),
  ('metabo_drill','Metabo','%Metabo Brushless%','BS 1OW-IK5','Cordless Drill Driver','voltage','12 V','battery_platform','12v-system','kit_contents','case'),
  ('raaco_shelving','Raaco','%Raaco Professional%','CarryLite 3CS-7A9','Shelving and Cabinet','storage_type','stacking box',NULL,NULL,NULL,NULL),
  ('keter_storage','Keter','%Keter Deep Stack%','2OO-VJU','Storage Bin and Organizer','storage_type','parts case','color_family','Yellow','volume','8 l'),
  ('fischer_anchor','Fischer','%Fischer SX%','SX 7UY-G03','Anchor and Wall Plug','anchor_type','concrete anchor','diameter','5 mm',NULL,NULL)
)
SELECT w.label, p.sku, p.path AS product_path, ts.store_id, ts.path AS store_path, i.available_today
FROM wanted w
JOIN products p
  ON p.brand = w.brand
 AND p.series LIKE w.series_like
 AND p.model = w.model
 AND p.name = w.pname
 AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.' || w.f1k) = w.f1v
 AND (w.f2k IS NULL OR json_extract(p.properties, '$.' || w.f2k) = w.f2v)
 AND (w.f3k IS NULL OR json_extract(p.properties, '$.' || w.f3k) = w.f3v)
JOIN target_store ts
LEFT JOIN inventory i
  ON i.store_id = ts.store_id AND i.sku = p.sku
ORDER BY w.label;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name,
       json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type') AS screw_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.diameter') AS diameter,
       json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type') AS fastener_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.cleaner_type') AS cleaner_type
FROM products
WHERE (
  brand='Heco' AND series='Heco Zinc Plated' AND model='TopFix GTU-YPJ' AND name='Wood and Drywall Screw'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw' AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='6 mm'
) OR (
  brand='Heco' AND series='Heco Zinc Plated' AND model='HECO 3DW-64B' AND name='Nut Bolt and Washer'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt' AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='10 mm'
) OR (
  brand='Ajax' AND series='Ajax Eco Classic' AND model='25C-DXE' AND name='Cleaning Liquid'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.cleaner_type')='degreaser'
) OR (
  brand='Heco' AND series='Heco Zinc Plated' AND model='TopFix GTU-YPJ' AND name='Wood and Drywall Screw'
  AND json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw' AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='3 mm'
)
LIMIT 20;

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

request.stdin: SELECT id, name, path FROM stores WHERE name LIKE '%Innsbruck%' OR name LIKE '%Wilten%';

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT 'store_innsbruck_wilten' AS store_id
)
SELECT 'bahco_pliers' AS item,
       p.sku,
       p.path,
       i.available_today
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku AND i.store_id = (SELECT store_id FROM target_store)
WHERE p.brand = 'Bahco'
  AND p.category = 'Pliers and Wrenches'
  AND p.series LIKE '%Bahco Heavy Duty%'
  AND p.model = 'ETS-3CM'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.tool_type') = 'side cutter'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.length') = '250 mm'
LIMIT 5

UNION ALL

SELECT 'spax_screw' AS item,
       p.sku,
       p.path,
       i.available_today
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku AND i.store_id = (SELECT store_id FROM target_store)
WHERE p.brand = 'Spax'
  AND p.category = 'Wood and Drywall Screw'
  AND p.series LIKE '%Spax Outdoor T-Star%'
  AND p.model = '1GS-NEI'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.screw_type') = 'drywall screw'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.diameter') = '6 mm'
LIMIT 5

UNION ALL

SELECT 'helios_paint' AS item,

       p.path,
       i.available_today
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku AND i.store_id = (SELECT store_id FROM target_store)
WHERE p.brand = 'Helios'
  AND p.category = 'Wall Paint'
  AND p.series LIKE '%Helios Quick Dry Mobihel%'
  AND p.model = '1VV-8Y1'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.color_family') = 'Green'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.finish') = 'eggshell'
LIMIT 5

UNION ALL

SELECT 'wavin_trap' AS item,
       p.sku,
       p.path,
       i.available_today
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku AND i.store_id = (SELECT store_id FROM target_store)
WHERE p.brand = 'Wavin'
  AND p.category = 'Drain Trap and Siphon'
  AND p.series LIKE '%Wavin Professional Tigris%'
  AND p.model = '31G-M1A'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.trap_type') = 'drain trap'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.diameter') = '25 mm'
LIMIT 5

UNION ALL

SELECT 'uvex_gloves' AS item,
       p.sku,
       p.path,
       i.available_today
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku AND i.store_id = (SELECT store_id FROM target_store)
WHERE p.brand = 'Uvex'
  AND p.category = 'Work Gloves'

  AND p.model = '2KV-937'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.size') = 'M'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.color_family') = 'Orange'
LIMIT 5

UNION ALL

SELECT 'mascot_trousers' AS item,
       p.sku,

LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku AND i.store_id = (SELECT store_id FROM target_store)

  AND p.model = '1GS-NEI'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.screw_type') = 'drywall screw'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.diameter') = '6 mm'
UNION ALL
SELECT 'helios_paint' AS item, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku AND i.store_id = 'store_innsbruck_wilten'
WHERE p.brand = 'Helios'
  AND p.name = 'Wall Paint'
  AND p.series LIKE '%Helios Quick Dry Mobihel%'
  AND p.model = '1VV-8Y1'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.color_family') = 'Green'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.finish') = 'eggshell'
UNION ALL
SELECT 'wavin_trap' AS item, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku AND i.store_id = 'store_innsbruck_wilten'
WHERE p.brand = 'Wavin'
  AND p.name = 'Drain Trap and Siphon'
  AND p.series LIKE '%Wavin Professional Tigris%'
  AND p.model = '31G-M1A'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.trap_type') = 'drain trap'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.diameter') = '25 mm'
UNION ALL
SELECT 'uvex_gloves' AS item, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku AND i.store_id = 'store_innsbruck_wilten'
WHERE p.brand = 'Uvex'
  AND p.name = 'Work Gloves'
  AND p.series LIKE '%Uvex Industrial uvex%'
  AND p.model = '2KV-937'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.size') = 'M'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.color_family') = 'Orange'
UNION ALL
SELECT 'mascot_trousers' AS item, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku AND i.store_id = 'store_innsbruck_wilten'
WHERE p.brand = 'Mascot'
  AND p.name = 'Work Trousers'
  AND p.series LIKE '%Mascot Pro ADV%'
  AND p.model = 'BAD-758'
  AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.color_family') = 'Yellow';

SELECT brand, model, series, name, sku, path, properties
FROM products
WHERE (brand='Bahco' AND model='ETS-3CM')
   OR (brand='Spax' AND model='1GS-NEI')
   OR (brand='Helios' AND model='1VV-8Y1')
   OR (brand='Wavin' AND model='31G-M1A')
   OR (brand='Uvex' AND model='2KV-937')
   OR (brand='Mascot' AND model='BAD-758')
LIMIT 50;

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.
